job description (2): As Chief Operating Officer of a 124-year-old, family-owned Italian American business, I work alongside our Russo family to honor and preserve our rich heritage while evolving our brand to thrive in today’s modern marketplace. My role bridges the “old world” values of tradition, craftsmanship, and community with the “new world” strategies of innovation, growth, and digital expansion. Working in partnership with the CEO, CFO, 4th , 5th generation owners and accounting team, I help diversify revenue streams, improve operational efficiency, and strengthen profitability while maintaining the authenticity and cultural legacy that define the Russo name. My leadership is centered on honoring tradition, building people, and creating innovative pathways for continued growth.
linkedin description (2): Our family’s roots trace back to Sicily as farmers, fishermen, and merchants, with a history in food and wine dating to the 1700s. In 1902, we established a small kiosk near Grand Rapids’ famed furniture factories, providing home-style meals for Italian artisans. By 1905, Giovanni Battista Russo and his parents, Francesco and Girolama, expanded to Wealthy Street, later acquiring a building at Division and Franklin in 1908. Family businesses flourished on the ground floor, with apartments above. At 752 S. Division, GB Russo Importer evolved into GB Russo & Son when Frank D. Russo was born in 1916. Giovanni and his daughter, Rose, also operated Roma Hall, a renowned venue that hosted legendary performers like Nat King Cole and The Supremes from the 1930s-70s. In the 1950s, GB and Frank opened the area’s first fast-food pizzeria at 750 Division, which later moved to 44th St. SW under cousin Michael Russo’s ownership. Frank married Mary Amante in 1947, and together they raised five children, including John F. Russo, who helped run the specialty food and wine store, moving locations over the decades before opening Carrettino Italian Market in the early 2000s. Now, John and his children—Gina, Mike, Greg, and Dominic—continue the family legacy with Russo Ristorante & Mercato at Tanger Outlets. This new format reflects the original S. Division location, blending authentic Italian cuisine, small plates, and fine wines in a casual setting. Our mercato offers premium Italian and Mediterranean groceries, sourced from family-owned, sustainable producers, alongside a curated selection of top American and European wines. Quality products and personal service remain our tradition!

summary: I discovered my passion for marketing unexpectedly when I convinced a leader in Disney Marketing to hire me for a special event project after stage-managing over 800 guest performances at Walt Disney World and working on over 50 special events for Disney Entertainment. Little did I know that this temporary assignment would lead to my fantastic career in branding, loyalty, marketing strategy, & partnerships. Today, I create marketing that leaves a legacy using my superpowers: Transforming brands: My proprietary Butler Branding BootcampTM process, refined over two decades, uncovers your unique brand & messaging through highly interactive sessions. Deepening customer loyalty: I optimize loyalty & membership programs to boost customer engagement, reduce acquisition costs, yield valuable customer data for future targeting & personalization, and drive revenue. Developing high-ROI marketing strategies: With a data-driven, results-oriented, & AI-first mindset, I align marketing & communication strategies with business objectives. From strategic annual marketing plans across owned/earned/paid channels to customer journey mapping, thought leadership, & content strategies, I ensure each marketing action contributes to business goals. I bring over two decades of growth marketing experience in B2B, B2C, & B2B2C to project-based & fractional Chief Marketing Officer engagements. Fostering connections: Finding hidden synergies & creating alliances by bringing together brands, ideas, & people to unlock new possibilities feeds my soul. From developing the first Disney Visa credit card & launching the program to forging strategic partnerships that add customer value & increase company revenue, my favorite career moments have centered around opportunities created when working together. Beyond the Boardroom - My personal passions: I won the friendly competition with my sisters to visit all 50 states first while on a business trip for JPMorgan Chase, proudly posing under the "Welcome to Arkansas" sign on I-65 South as semi-trucks sped by. My love for travel, which started during family summer vacations, driving in our station wagon to national parks and historic sites, continues to motivate me today. Whether it's escaping on a weekend getaway to listen to great music & eat great food or planning my next overseas adventure, I'm always ready to explore. Travel has shaped my career choices & fuels my passion for what I do. Ask me my favorite bourbon cocktail by city. Or share yours! I enjoy connecting & would love to hear from you. summary: Every growing company has blind spots, but they’re not obstacles. They’re opportunities to unlock insight. 🔍🔓🔑 Is your path to scale unclear, your traction inconsistent? I work with founders and leadership teams to eliminate barriers to growth, optimizing sales, marketing, customer success and revenue operations to drive results. 𝗪𝗵𝗼 𝗔𝗺 𝗜? With experience founding three companies and 20+ years in sales and marketing, I understand what it takes to build lean and scale with confidence. Originally a digital media agency founder, I later led global automotive industry strategy at Google, growing a $1B business by 25%. In ed tech and workforce training, I helped create economic mobility for underserved populations, and testified before the U.S. House of Representatives. I founded Mission Flywheel to help leaders eliminate blind spots, and turn revenue strategy into measurable results. 𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗗𝗼 𝗜 𝗗𝗼? As a revenue strategy partner to early-stage and growth-stage companies, I help them achieve their goals by augmenting five key business functions: sales, business development, customer success, marketing and revenue operations. Every success story traces back to three key pillars: 🚀 𝗙𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗟𝗶𝗳𝘁: elevating pure automation to business acceleration 🎯 𝗢𝘂𝘁𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗲𝘀 𝗔𝗹𝗶𝗴𝗻𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁: translating capability and potential into performance 🧠 𝗛𝘂𝗺𝗮𝗻 𝗘𝗻𝗮𝗯𝗹𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁: utilizing AI as a lever to maximize human potential Together we turn strategy into action, and build a business that doesn’t just survive change, but thrives in it. 𝗪𝗵𝘆 𝗗𝗼 𝗜 𝗗𝗼 𝗜𝘁? Management guru Peter Drucker famously said: “The best way to predict the future is to create it.” And celebrated entrepreneur and philanthropist Marc Benioff added: “Business is the greatest platform for change.” I believe business should unlock the best in people, focus energy on solving real problems, and do it all with care for the finite resources we depend on. That’s the kind of future I want to help create. summary: I’m a direct response marketer, former pro hockey coach, EMDR and Ray Peat Nutrition advocate, who's caught up in the adventure of helping restless children and exhausted first responders find better sleep. linkedin description: Our mission is to help exhausted parents and restless children around the world with a unique sleep solution. Since introducing our beds to so many families, the feedback has been overwhelming with relief, hope, and excitement. With each delivery of a zPod, we feel like pages are turning and new chapters are beginning. As a parent, imagine a time where your child was struggling. It was probably challenging to see your child struggle… especially when you feel powerless and do not have the answers. We understand how that feels. We’ve seen children sleeping in their closets, under their beds, or barely at all. And when your child doesn’t sleep well, you don’t sleep well. After receiving a zPod, we have children that went from sleeping only 3 hours a night to 11 hours per night. These stories are real. We feel them everyday as so many parents are looking for answers. As a new chapter begins when trying one of our beds, families are finding a belief that something different can happen. And as this belief continues to grow, it gives families all over the world a belief that they can begin a new chapter as well. The chapter of better rest, less exhaustion, and more love in the household. If you are a DME dealer and would like to partner with us to help our customers, or add zPods into one of your settings then please message us: support@zpodsforsleep.com Or, Message @Gary Kellmann. -The zPods Family

summary: Most service businesses don’t struggle because they aren’t trying hard enough. They’ve reached a point where sales and marketing can’t keep running on different playbooks. Results feel inconsistent. Deals take too long. Sales isn't talking to enough right fit prospects. Marketing activity increases—but revenue doesn’t follow. That’s usually when leaders start asking better questions: → What’s actually driving growth here? → What needs to change—and what’s just noise? The answer isn’t more effort. It’s better strategic alignment. That’s where I come in. I’ve spent over a decade advising leadership teams across financial services, professional services, and B2B organizations—helping them make sense of complexity and focus on what actually drives growth. What I Help Businesses Navigate I work primarily with B2B and professional service firms at key growth inflection points, including: - Scaling beyond founder-led sales - Clarifying positioning as the business matures - Moving from opportunistic growth to intentional growth - Preparing for acquisition, merger, or exit A core specialty of my work is helping businesses realign sales and marketing when misalignment is costing momentum, margin, or morale. How I Work - Strategic assessments and marketing roadmaps - On-site and virtual workshops for leadership teams - Fractional strategic advisory (fCMO) partnerships Many of our clients operate on EOS or work with a Pinnacle guide or business coach. They’ve done the foundational work—but now need a clear, practical sales and marketing strategy that aligns everyone around a simple, effective plan for growth. This isn’t about adding another framework. It’s about translating leadership vision into focused execution teams can rally around. The Results Clients Experience - Clear positioning and messaging sales can confidently lead with - Buyers who are already aligned before the first sales conversation - Faster, higher-value sales conversations with fewer wrong-fit prospects - Less wasted sales effort and shorter sales cycles - Reduced friction between teams - Greater confidence in growth decisions We intentionally work with a small number of right-fit clients each year. When we’re not the best fit, we refer businesses to trusted strategic advisors from our vetted network. Our process and pricing are transparent and clearly outlined on our website. I also train experienced marketers to step into high-value strategic advisory roles, helping them move beyond execution and lead at a level that truly impacts results.

Since then, we’ve expanded into the St. Louis market and evolved into a full-service agency leading social, influencer, and digital marketing campaigns across tourism, lifestyle, and consumer-based industries. My path wasn’t linear. I oversee everything from strategy to systems. linkedin description (3): That GenZ Agency is a boutique growth partner built to help brands stop guessing what modern marketing is supposed to look like and start winning attention that actually converts. The focus is not on selling posts, ads, or surface-level “Gen Z vibes,” but on creating cultural relevance with a direct connection to revenue. The agency operates in the gap where established brands, private equity–backed portfolios, and experience-driven companies already have budget and product–market fit, yet lack the speed, platform-native creative, and cultural instincts required to stay competitive in today’s attention economy. Rather than functioning as a traditional marketing vendor, the agency integrates as an embedded growth function. Its role sits close to the decisions that shape demand — positioning, messaging, creative direction, distribution strategy, and the feedback systems that reveal what is resonating in real time. The emphasis shifts away from deliverables and toward building a modern demand engine tailored to each brand. While the work often includes UGC-style creative, social-native storytelling, and performance distribution, the real value lies in the system that continuously tests, learns, and evolves behind the scenes. At a higher level, the agency serves as a translation layer between traditional brand operations and the way people discover, build trust, and make purchasing decisions today. Cultural signals are identified early, shaped into creative that feels current and native to each platform, and connected to distribution channels designed to drive measurable business outcomes. Relevance is built intentionally, amplified through the right platforms, and converted into sustained growth — all within a long-term partnership model that evolves alongside the brand rather than running campaigns in the background.

summary: Turning a lifelong love for waterways, marketing, and technology into a company! Proud co-founder of Ballyhoo Media, a family-owned company innovating the maritime and out-of-home advertising industry by uniquely connecting brands with people on America's waterways. We started this company with little more than a love for the water and an audacious idea. Zero knowledge of outdoor advertising or boats? No problem. It turns out that sometimes, naivety plus a giant screen on a boat can be a recipe for success. Since setting sail in 2016, Ballyhoo Media has become the world's leading water-based media company. Our fleet of American-made digital billboard boats has empowered businesses, from Fortune 500s to local storefronts, to make waves with unique advertising and experiential campaigns. Beyond our boats, we've teamed up with hundreds of vessels nationwide, creating unforgettable activations and bringing unique forms of advertising to waterfront communities like never before. But our platforms aren't the only thing that makes us unique. Ballyhoo Media was born from our love for the waterways, and our mission stretches beyond advertising. We're committed to enhancing our waters, organizing unforgettable events, supporting local businesses, and joining hands with communities and local governments. We stand firm in preserving and protecting our oceans, adopting "blue technologies" in our boats, and spreading non-profit messaging, all to ensure our waterways are around for future generations to enjoy. Now, we're harnessing our unique expertise in waterway advertising and technologies to spearhead further innovation within the passenger vessel industry. Our goal? To inject new life into the industry and enrich the passenger experience on ferries, water taxis, and tour boats across America. At Ballyhoo Media, we envision the water as an untapped canvas, a space for meaningful communication, and we're excited about the waves we're creating in this arena. When I'm not rocking a bathing suit and making a splash with Ballyhoo Media, you catch me soaking up live music with my best friend, Don Julio, watching my Baltimore Ravens on the road to a super bowl, cruising top-down in my Ford Bronco, and always saying 'yes' to weekend plans.

education school linkedin description (2: Christian Brothers High School is a Lasallian Catholic coeducational college preparatory high school. We are dedicated to inspiring and empowering young men and women with a challenging, relevant, and diverse academic program and co-curricular activities. Students thrive in a Christ-centered community that fosters faith, integrity, global citizenship, leadership and service, preparing students for college and life. Take a walk on the Christian Brothers High School campus and the feeling of community among the students, teachers and staff is palpable. In its 140 years in Sacramento, this venerable high school has changed its location three times and been known by six different names. What remains unchanged is the inimitable spirit—the Lasallian spirit and tradition of teaching and caring for young people in a community steadfastly dedicated to the intellectual growth and spiritual well-being of its students. This rich tradition of Lasallian education dates back to 1680 when St. John Baptist de La Salle, a prominent French priest, founded the Brothers of the Christian Schools “to give a human and Christian education to the young, especially the poor and working class.” De La Salle transformed education by forming a community of educators with whom he developed a spirituality of teaching and learning. In 1876, the De La Salle Christian Brothers and members of the Catholic community brought that legacy to the Sacramento valley establishing the St. Patrick Institute at 12th and K streets, evolving into what is now Christian Brothers High School. Today, more than 1,100 young men and women enter its doors each day and each, in his or her own way, continues the legacy of learning, service and inclusive community that are at the very heart of a CB education.

summary: I help dispensaries stop bleeding customers. After 15+ years in creative and a decade building companies, I landed in cannabis in 2018 and saw a pattern: great shops losing loyal customers while relying on loyalty tools that didn’t actually solve the problem. I helped Michigan cannabis operators starting in 2018 when they started to go legal Rec and saw the rise and fall of those stores in the following years. I saw owners and investors I became friends with lose it all and get crushed. It was devastating. Dispensary owners/managers would ask "how do we get more customers?" and then I looked at their data and realized they had tens of thousands of customers that had already come through their doors, and we had emails for a lot of them. That started my obsession to crack retention... That’s why I started Tact — the only agency built 100% around dispensary retention. No fluff. No vanity metrics. Just systems that bring people back and revenue that sticks. We actively work with 70+ stores today and have consulted with 150+ stores, from single-location operators to MSOs, and I’ve seen the inside of more dispensary data dashboards than I can count. summary: I’ve spent the last 20+ years helping agencies win business and learning firsthand what actually works. I started my first company in high school, building a concert promotions business that grew into a multimillion-dollar experiential marketing firm. Along the way, we worked with Grammy Award–winning artists like Lady Gaga and Pitbull and activated brand programs for Disney, Toyota, and Anheuser-Busch. Then the 2008 recession hit. Like many agencies, we had grown on referrals and reputation, and when demand stopped, so did the pipeline. That experience pushed me deep into business development. I sold my agency, rebuilt my skill set, and spent the next decade leading new business and growth programs at independent and holding company agencies. I’ve helped agencies generate tens of millions of dollars in opportunities and win work with brands like Kohl’s, Constellation Brands, Netflix, Heineken, and Autodesk. During the pandemic, history repeated itself—agencies losing clients, pipelines drying up. This time, I knew how to help. I left a senior agency role and founded NextBigWin to help agencies see opportunity earlier, earn trust sooner, and stop competing only at the RFP stage. Today, in addition to leading NextBigWin, I host "CMO Journeys," a video interview series featuring senior marketing leaders who discuss their perspectives on growth, partnerships, and agency relationships. I am a member of the 4A’s Expert Network, where I advise agencies on modern, signal-driven growth strategies. If you run or lead growth at an agency selling high-consideration services and want better timing, better access, and fewer cold starts, I’m always open to a conversation.
job description: I founded NextBigWin (formerly Christian Banach Growth Consulting) to help agencies engage buyers earlier—before decisions take shape. linkedin description: 𝗡𝗲𝘅𝘁𝗕𝗶𝗴𝗪𝗶𝗻 (formerly Christian Banach Growth Consulting) is an intelligence and access platform built to help agencies engage the right companies earlier—before buying decisions take shape. Most agencies don’t struggle because of talent. They struggle because meaningful access happens too late. By the time an RFP is issued or a shortlist forms, relationships already exist and influence is limited. NextBigWin helps agencies see opportunity sooner and earn relevance before decisions are locked in. We track early buying signals that often precede agency evaluations, including CMO and leadership changes, funding and M&A activity, strategic shifts, and both public and private RFPs. summary: Most organizations think they have a brand. What they actually have is a logo, a mission statement, and a marketing budget — which makes them a commodity with good packaging. There's no shame in that. It's where most organizations live. But it's expensive to stay there — because commodities compete on price, volume, and hope. Brands compete on meaning. And meaning wins. I'm here to serve mission-driven leaders who suspect there's a gear they haven't found yet. The ones building something that matters — and who sense that the distance between where they are and where they should be isn't simply a marketing problem. Spoiler alert: It's a strategy problem. As your embedded Fractional Chief Strategy Officer, I don't just parachute in with a deck and disappear. We will (together) identify your strategy, innovate and integrate it across every function of your organization — operations, culture, delivery, engagement — and provide ongoing leadership to protect and scale it. Not as a communications exercise. As an organizational discipline. The result? Differentiation that sticks. Loyalty that compounds. Valuation that reflects what you've actually built. We've done this for 15+ organizations across healthcare, financial services, wine, nonprofits, startups, and beyond — building, tightening, defending, and scaling strategies that turn purpose into market position. If you're tired of being the best-kept secret in your category — or worse, being seen as interchangeable with your competitors — that's the gap we close. Send me a DM and let's book a discovery call. We'll find out together whether you're a brand or a commodity. And if you're not sure, that's exactly where we start.

job description: Here's an uncomfortable question: Are you a brand, or a commodity in a brand wrapper? Most leaders answer quickly. The honest answer takes longer — because real brand isn't what you say about yourself. It's whether your strategy makes you the only choice for the people who matter most to your mission. Corridor Strategy Group is a fractional Chief Strategy Officer practice for mission-driven organizations that are done treating strategy like a subset of marketing. Because that's how brands become commodities — slowly, then all at once. What started as an award-winning brand strategy and creative firm serving disruptors evolved into something more fundamental: embedded strategy leadership. Today, we serve as your ongoing CSO — identifying, building, and integrating strategy across your entire organization. Not just your messaging. Your operations. Your culture. Your innovations. The decisions you make when no one's watching. That's where strategy actually lives. We've led this work for 15+ organizations — startups finding their footing, turnarounds finding their identity, and purpose-driven companies across healthcare, financial services, hospitality, nonprofits, and more — all discovering that the distance between commodity and brand isn't a budget gap. summary: From Army Sergeant Major to 3x LinkedIn Top Voice, Forbes Coaches Council and Senior Manager at EY, my transition from the military to the private sector must seem idyllic. It was (in fact) the hardest year of my life - and that is why I became so active on LinkedIn + created HireMilitary, Tenova LLC and the Digital Networking Professional Program. HireMilitary is the ONLY veteran staffing company out there that provides Direct Placement (veterans & military spouses), but ALSO leverages the DoD SkillBridge Program to bring interns or “fellows” to companies for 3-4 month of OJT where the company is not allowed to pay salary or benefits (FLSA compliant) because the military still covers it. We bring an unparalleled social media & outreach program that touches more than 2.2M people every month in support of your requirements. The Digital Networking Professional Program trains & certifies individuals to leverage LinkedIn in support of personal or corporate branding, growing a network, engaging with content and leveraging their free tools. The Digital Networking Coach certificate gets after those “optimizers” that charge people, but don’t have any way to back up their LinkedIn or coaching experience in a way that matters to potential clients. We are an approved Army Training Vendor for this under credentialing assistance. Tenova LLC is a service disabled veteran owned small business (SDVOSB) that brings the unique quals of HireMilitary & the Digital Networking Professional Program, couples them with training, communications, and change management, to support government & commercial clients of all sizes. I teach the transitioning General and Flag Officers from every branch of service, am a regular public speaker, train businesses on leveraging LinkedIn or Veteran Hiring Programs & volunteer much of my time supporting the military community both on and off-base. linkedin description (3): The U.S. Department of State is focused on accomplishing America's mission of diplomacy at home and around the world. The U.S. Department of State manages America’s relationships with foreign governments, international organizations, and the people of other countries. U.S. diplomats and Civil Service professionals carry out the President’s foreign policy and help build a more free, prosperous, and secure world. U.S. Department of State employees, with their skills, character and commitment to public service, are the backbone of America's diplomacy. They represent the people and advocate the interests of the U.S. to the rest of the world. The Foreign Service (FS) is dedicated to representing America and responding to the needs of American citizens in other countries. Members of the Foreign Service can be sent to any embassy, consulate, or other diplomatic mission anywhere in the world, at any time, to serve the diplomatic needs of the United States. Civil Service (CS) employees provide in-depth expertise and continuity in accomplishing all aspects of the Department's mission and are involved in virtually every function of the Department. Civil Service employees issue passports, compile and analyze overseas reports, provide logistical support to posts, consult with Congress about foreign policy initiatives and policies, and formulate and manage the budget. Nearly 7,400 Civil Service (CS) employees serve as a stable resource pool in Washington D.C., at Passport Agencies and field offices throughout the United States, and at several Consulates in Mexico. More than 31,000 Foreign Service National (FSN) employees also supplement the personnel requirements of the Department overseas. FSN employees are local residents of the countries in which we maintain diplomatic posts and consulates. They are valued members of our embassy teams, for they provide continuity for the transient American staff and have language and cultural expertise.

summary: I built Customer Success as a growth mechanism. The goal was always revenue — specifically, getting more of it from customers companies had already acquired. Not to make customers happy. Not to reduce churn as an end in itself. To get customers to outcomes that matter to their business, because that's the only model where the commercial results actually follow. I formalized the discipline. Co-authored the book (Wiley, 2016). Watched the field spend a decade trying to pretend CS wasn't commercial. Took the message to sales leaders and CROs instead, framed it in revenue terms, and found the same universal problem everywhere: companies with customer bases full of untapped lifetime value and no system to capture it. That's what I've been building since. LTV:Max is the framework and resources for maximizing customer lifetime value systematically. Extensible Agents is what CS becomes when agentic AI removes the bottleneck that was always the customer — and frees your team to do the work only humans can do. The same inflection point that happened in 2012, when Customer Success went from instinct to discipline, is happening again. The teams that formalize agentic CS first will define what this function looks like for the next decade. If you run CS at a SaaS company and you're trying to figure out what this means for your team: sixteenventures.com, ltvmax.com, extensibleagents.com.
job description: For 15 years, Customer Success organizations were told what they could and could not do. By the systems they were given access to. By the gatekeepers who controlled those systems. By their own beliefs about what was possible. That sentence — "I wish we could just do that for the customer, but we just can't" — just became obsolete. Extensible Agents is built for CS teams who are ready to stop conforming to systems that were never built for them. We build agentic workflows that absorb the complexity customers were never going to handle themselves, free CS teams to do the work only humans can do, and ask not what is the least we can do for a customer — but what is the most. The same inflection point that happened in 2012, when Customer Success went from an instinct to a discipline, is happening again. The teams that formalize agentic CS first will define what this function looks like for the next decade. We built this company for those teams.

summary: Growth doesn’t happen by accident. It happens when leadership, culture, strategy - and the right relationships - move in sync. I’m Illianna Acosta, Founder & CEO at LeadWell Ventures. I help leaders build businesses that scale by aligning people, partnerships, and performance around a clear growth strategy.Before launching LeadWell, I spent 20+ years in AdTech, media sales, and partnerships, most recently as Head of Enterprise AdTech Partnerships, North America at LinkedIn. There, I helped build and scale LinkedIn’s enterprise partner ecosystem, aligning global technology partners, agencies, and internal stakeholders to drive measurable revenue growth.That experience shaped how I see business: sustainable growth is rarely about a single tactic. It’s about building ecosystems — internally (your leadership and culture) and externally (your partnerships and market relationships). Today, I help leaders do exactly that.At LeadWell Ventures, I work with founders, emerging leaders, and executive teams to:• Align leadership and company strategy so decisions flow from vision, not reaction• Build scalable systems and strategic relationship ecosystems that create long-term growth• Develop emotionally intelligent leaders who inspire trust, accountability, and performanceMy work is grounded in equity, authenticity, and human potential - principles I lived as Global Co-Chair of LinkedIn’s Hispanic ERG (HOLA) and continue to bring into leadership development and coaching.I’m also a certified coach, a startup advisor, angel investor, keynote speaker, and author of Lost in Translation, where I explore leadership, career growth, and the intersection of identity and ambition.Because scaling a company isn’t just about revenue. It’s about building something, and someone, that lasts.

linkedin description: LeadWell Ventures is a leadership and growth consultancy helping mid-market B2B companies build the human capability that drives sustainable revenue growth. We work at the intersection of strategy, leadership, and commercial execution - partnering with executive teams and revenue organizations to close the gap between a strong go-to-market strategy and the teams capable of executing it. Mid-market companies face a unique challenge. You're past the startup stage, but you don't have the budgets or infrastructure of a large enterprise. You need senior-level thinking, practical execution, and a partner who moves at your pace. That's exactly what LeadWell delivers. Our work spans three areas: Strategic Growth Consultancy - Partner ecosystem strategy, co-sell architecture, and GTM design for organizations ready to turn partner relationships into a predictable revenue engine. Sales & Leadership Enablement - Training and enablement programs built around your team's real deal cycles and leadership challenges - not a generic curriculum delivered off the shelf. Executive Coaching - 1:1 coaching for founders and senior leaders navigating growth, transition, and the evolving demands of high-performance leadership. LeadWell was founded by Illianna Acosta - a leadership architect, certified executive coach, and enterprise growth advisor with 20+ years of experience, including seven years at LinkedIn leading strategic partner initiatives across a $5.2B revenue ecosystem. The organizations that consistently outperform their peers are not simply the ones with the best products or strategies. They are the ones that invest deeply in leadership capability. That's what we build. summary: I help leaders, businesses, and communities build what they can see — but can’t always explain. summary: Imagine your leadership team as a genuine engine for growth. Aligned, collaborative, and moving fast in the same direction. Leaders who trust each other, challenge each other, and show up every day as the kind of leaders others want to work with and for. That's not a nice-to-have. For a high-growth company, it's the difference between hitting your targets and wondering why you're working so hard to hold everything together. Most leadership teams aren't there yet, not because they lack talent, but because exceptional people don't automatically become an exceptional team. That gap is where growth stalls. 𝗪𝗛𝗢 𝗬𝗢𝗨 𝗔𝗥𝗘 🔸 CEO with aggressive targets and a team that looks aligned on the surface, but isn't 🔸 Chief People Officer who owns leadership development and knows the team dynamics are costing you performance 🔸 COO responsible for execution efficiency and watching it suffer 𝗛𝗢𝗪 𝗜 𝗛𝗘𝗟𝗣 I guide executive teams through The Compass Team Experience, a structured, sequenced leadership development program built for high-growth companies. Most approaches address team dynamics or individual leadership behavior. That's why they don't stick. Real change requires both, in parallel and in the right sequence. The right intervention at the wrong time doesn't just fail — it sets teams back.
